global_instructions: You are writing sections of a financial advice report addressed to the client. Write in clear, professional British English, always in the first-person plural ('we', 'our', 'us'). Never use first-person singular ('I', 'me', 'my'). Output only the text for the section itself, with no extra headings or commentary.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.template:
Further to our recent discussions, we are writing to set out our advice in relation to <<scope>>. This advice is based on the information currently available to us.

This firm is authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority.

scope.prompt: Output a brief noun phrase naming the accounts this report covers, derived from the 'Accounts covered' field in the report request document. Include every account type listed there — do not omit any, including any new account mentioned even if it does not yet appear in the account database. Join the final item with 'and' (e.g. '...joint GIAs, and the Meridian offshore bond'). Do not write a full sentence — the phrase slots into 'we are writing to set out our advice in relation to [your output]', so it must read as a continuation of that sentence.

sections.title: Background & Objectives
sections.template:
<<summary>>

The accounts covered by this report are as follows:

<<holdings_table>>

summary.prompt: Summarise the client's objectives and circumstances in prose only. Do not include any account tables, account lists, or account values — those appear in the holdings table that follows this text. Do not include specific monetary amounts such as inheritance figures, sale proceeds, new money, or any transaction amounts — these belong in Recommendations. Do not reference specific account performance, valuations, or figures from meeting notes; focus on the client's goals, risk profile, and personal circumstances only.

holdings_table.prompt: You have been given a baseline holdings table built deterministically from the account database, which is the system of record for account values. Do not replace any value in the table unless ALL of the following are true: (1) the replacement figure is exact and precise — reject any figure qualified with words such as 'around', 'approximately', 'a little over', 'roughly', 'about', or similar; (2) it comes from a formal platform statement or official valuation document — not from meeting notes, adviser file notes, or any informal source; (3) its date is explicitly stated and is strictly later than the valuation_date for that account shown in the account data. If you are uncertain whether any condition is met, keep the baseline figure unchanged. Exclude any account whose status is shown as 'closed' in the account data. Preserve any valuation date annotations (for example '(valued 28 Feb 2026)') that appear in the baseline table — do not remove or alter them. Output only the final markdown table, with no extra commentary.

recommendation.prompt: Describe what the client should do with their investments and why, including the specific amounts involved and any platform charges relevant to the recommendation. Follow this structure exactly: (1) Pool all sources of funds together first — include only funds that have already been received and are immediately available for investment. Exclude any contingent, deferred, or not-yet-received amounts such as earnouts. Deduct any committed repayments or loan repayments stated in the source documents before stating the net investable total. Disinvestment proceeds and any new money are combined into a single total before any allocation is made. Do not add disinvestment proceeds again after the allocation; they are already counted in the total. Do not subtract or deduct disinvestment proceeds from the remaining balance at any point — they are a source of funds already counted in the total pool, not an outflow. (2) State the net investable total and how each source contributes to it. (3) Allocate from that pool: use the ISA headroom figures from the isa_headroom section above as the maximum new contribution per ISA — do not use the current ISA account value as the contribution amount. ISA headroom is a cap on how much can be contributed, not a source of funds — do not add ISA headroom to the pool total. (4) After ISA contributions, allocate to all remaining products listed in the 'Product recommended' field of the report request in turn — for example SIPP top-ups, contributions to existing accounts — before calculating any remainder. Only allocate to accounts explicitly named in the 'Product recommended' field — do not infer additional accounts from meeting notes or other sources. Where 'Product recommended' names an account type that exists more than once in the portfolio, identify the correct account by matching the platform name in 'Selling existing investments?' against the account database to find the specific account ID — reference only that account ID and do not include any other account of the same type. If a contribution amount is unknown and cannot be derived from the source documents, write '<<insert value here>>' and do not attempt to calculate or state a running balance for that step — instead state that the remaining balance will be confirmed once the figure is finalised. Do not use '<<insert value here>>' for amounts that can be calculated from the net investable total and the known allocations — state the calculated figure instead. If the report request names a product but the amount is not available in the source documents, write '<<insert value here>>' for that figure rather than omitting the product or inventing a figure. (5) Only after all named products are accounted for does the remaining balance go to any new account. For any account values, use only the figures from the account database — do not use figures from meeting notes or any other informal source. When stating ISA contribution amounts, note that the headroom figures assume no contributions have already been made in the current tax year — if the source documents indicate the ISA is already part-funded, state that the contribution uses the remaining allowance rather than the full headroom. Do not reference fees, platform charges, or ongoing charges in this section — these are covered in the Fees & Charges section. Do not mention capital gains tax, tax liabilities, or any tax implications — those are covered in a separate section.

sections.title: Tax Implications
sections.use_if: Include this section when the recommendation involves selling investments that could be taxable.
sections.template: Please be aware that <<disposal_description>> may give rise to a capital gains tax liability. Any gain arising will be assessed against the annual exempt amount.

disposal_description.prompt: Using the 'Selling existing investments?' field from the report request as your source, write a brief noun phrase describing the disposal — for example 'a partial disposal of the Holloway joint General Investment Account'. Include the platform name if there is more than one account of the same type. Reflect whether the disposal is partial or full as stated in the report request. Output only the noun phrase — no full sentence, no commentary, no punctuation at the end.

fees.prompt: Set out all fees and charges that apply to the client's investments, including any initial charge and the ongoing charges (platform charge and ongoing advice charge). If a specific figure is not available in the source documents, write '<<insert value here>>' for that line rather than omitting it or inventing a figure.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.template:
The value of investments can fall as well as rise and you may get back less than you invest. Past performance is not a guide to future returns.

If you are happy to proceed with our recommendations, please let us know and we will implement them for you.
